rulings.Case Title: OMAR BARSTAD et al VS. LAMONS GASKET COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, April 13, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to Issues 1-4 and granted as to Issue 5. As to summary judgment and summary adjudication Issues 1-4, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant and that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that any exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to the risk of developing his asbestos-related disease. Plaintiff's responses to defendant's special discovery and Earl Daniel's deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to summary adjudication Issue 5 (punitive damages), defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiff failed to present clear and convincing evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. The Court already heard argument on the issues of threshold exposure to asbestos and substantial factor causation and no further argument will be allowed on those issues, however the Court put this motion back on calendar to give the parties the opportunity to address the motion for summary adjudication, as such opportunity was not afforded to the parties at the April 9, 2015 hearing. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:00a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
